title: thlaspi praecox subsp. cuneifolium
intro: Thlaspi praecox subsp. cuneifolium, also known as early cress or cress, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Thlaspi praecox subsp. cuneifolium include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory issues, and provide antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, and can also be eaten raw in salads or used as a garnish.
